FP&A Analyst

An exciting opportunity for an FP&A Analyst to join the finance team of this global business. Based in Sydney, you will play a key role in delivering financial analysis and insights to support strategic decision-making across the organisation.

You will be responsible for supporting the budgeting and forecasting process, preparing monthly reporting, and delivering ad-hoc analysis for senior management. The role also offers exposure to corporate development initiatives, providing a rare opportunity to gain insight into complex deal structures and strategic transactions.

Key Responsibilities:

Financial and management reporting activities.

Assist with the preparation and consolidation of budget submissions, including the group budget process.

Provide financial analysis and insights to support business decision-making.

Provide support on M&A and corporate development initiatives.

Deliver ad-hoc analysis and reporting for senior management and key stakeholders.

Liaise with the external audit team and support technical accounting matters.

About you:

Experience from within Big 4 or mid-tier firms across Audit, M&A, Transaction Services or Corporate Advisory highly regarded.

Strong analytical capability with the ability to interpret financial data and provide meaningful insights.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage with stakeholders across the business.

Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities.

What’s on offer?

Rare opportunity to join a global organisation.

Exposure to M&A and corporate development activities.

Work alongside a highly capable and experienced finance team.

A role offering strong autonomy, responsibility, and excellent career development.

Life at Steadfast

Steadfast is built on a simple belief: none of us are as good as all of us. It shapes how we work, how we support our brokers and partners, and how we show up for each other every day.

Our Together values guide everything we do, we listen, we collaborate, we include, and we back each other to succeed. As Australia’s largest general insurance broker network, we help businesses and communities feel supported, protected and confident in the moments that matter. When you join Steadfast, you become part of a team where your ideas are welcomed, your wellbeing matters, and your growth is genuinely encouraged.

Your new role at Steadfast

We are looking for a Pricing Actuary who enjoys solving problems, creating clarity, supporting others, improving systems and building strong relationships. You will bring curiosity, care and a collaborative mindset to your work.

You will work closely with Miramar, Steadfast Group, Risk, Compliance and key business stakeholders to strengthen pricing processes, deliver insights, support decision‑making and improve experiences. You will bring your technical expertise and, just as importantly, your ability to listen, collaborate and create an environment where people feel comfortable sharing ideas and raising challenges early. In doing this work, you’ll naturally embody our Together values, creating a culture where people feel heard, included and supported to do their best work

What your days may look like

You may start your morning checking in with Miramar and internal partners, reviewing priorities and identifying emerging challenges. You might be facilitating a pricing review, helping teams connect the dots and make confident decisions.

By midday, you could be deep in actuarial modelling, analysis or portfolio conversations, translating complexity into clarity and shaping insights that help the business move forward. In the afternoon, you might be partnering with delivery teams, leaders or product owners to refine a model, enhance a pricing tool or map out the next steps of an initiative.

No two days look the same, but every day you will be contributing to work that has real impact across our network and the communities we support.

The work you will lead and shape

You’ll have the opportunity to:

Create: Build and refine pricing models, frameworks and analytical tools that strengthen how we work.
Collaborate: Bring together diverse perspectives across Miramar, actuarial, risk, compliance and business teams.
Challenge: Ask thoughtful questions, test assumptions and help us make better pricing and portfolio decisions.
Deliver: Own key pricing work and see it through to meaningful commercial outcomes.
Support: Provide guidance, clarity and confidence to stakeholders during reviews and decision‑making processes.

You may also:

Develop pricing frameworks, modelling tools and actuarial processes.
Maintain reporting systems, data frameworks and actuarial documentation.
Build capability, relationships and insights across the business.
Lead pricing initiatives, analysis and continuous improvements.
Prepare reports, recommendations and governance papers.

What You will Bring

Experience in actuarial pricing, general insurance or a related field, and confidence working with actuarial systems and modelling environments.
Strength in analysis, communication, stakeholder engagement and problem solving.
Ability to translate complex actuarial concepts into clear, practical insights for non‑technical stakeholders.
A collaborative mindset and the ability to build trusted relationships.
Relevant qualifications in Actuarial Studies, Mathematics, Statistics or equivalent experience.

If you are not sure you meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply, we value potential and diverse experiences.

Why Steadfast?

You will join a team that genuinely believes we are stronger together. You will be supported by leaders who want you to grow, colleagues who value your perspective, and a culture built on respect, inclusion and shared success. Here, your contribution matters and your voice is heard.

Our benefits program is built to support you through the moments that matter most, whether you're at work, at home, or anywhere life takes you. This includes generous leave entitlements, flexible work options, wellbeing resources, an employee assistance program and a range of insurance offerings that help you feel supported and protected. You will also have access to meaningful learning and development opportunities that help you build capability, explore new ideas and grow your career with confidence.

Most importantly, you will be joining a workplace where people feel safe to be themselves. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and experiences because we know that when we bring different perspectives together, we do our best work and create a place where everyone can thrive.

Steadfast Group Limited and the Steadfast entity recruiting for this role (“we/us”) collect your personal information to assess your application, communicate with you, conduct police checks (for all hires) and other role-specific checks where relevant and lawful, and if successful, progress onboarding. If you do not provide requested information, we may not be able to progress your application. 

If your application is unsuccessful, we may keep your details and contact you about future opportunities unless you opt out; you can opt out at any time by contacting talent@steadfast.com.au. 

We may share information with Steadfast related entities and service providers supporting recruitment (e.g., screening/verification providers, agencies, and technology providers). Information may be stored or processed in Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and the Philippines (which may change over time).

For privacy access/correction requests or complaints contact the Privacy Officer at privacyofficer@steadfast.com.au or +61 2 9495 6557.

Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About the Team

At Workday, we believe that the happiness, development, and contribution of every Workmate is central to who we are. Our ANZ business has seen significant growth in the last four years, and we expect this to accelerate as the region continues to transform.

As a member of our diverse and skilled Presales team, you will sit at the intersection of Sales, Product Management, Consulting, and Marketing. Your mission is to bring the value of Workday solutions to life, showcasing how the office of the CFO and CHRO can be revolutionized through next-generation SaaS planning and consolidation applications.

About the Role

Workday is looking for a senior-level technical professional with deep expertise in both Planning and Consolidation. You will leverage your product and industry knowledge to provide functional expertise throughout the sales process for our largest enterprise prospects.

You will lead discovery workshops with prospective customers to uncover

business requirements and value. You will provide authoritative overview and deep-dive product demonstrations and position the unique value of Workday Adaptive Planning. You will conduct executive-level presentations and provide trusted advice and mentorship as a planning subject matter expert. We also assist in responding to RFP’s, participate in Workday events such as webinars, customer forums, andindustry conferences and tradeshows.

Key Responsibilities:

Strategic Discovery: Lead workshops with prospective customers to uncover business requirements, current pain points, and potential value.

High-Impact Demonstrations/Presentations: Deliver authoritative deep-dive product demonstrations and executive-level presentations that position the unique value of Workday Adaptive Planning.

Technical Excellence: Develop and champion Workday-based solutions, which may include rapid prototyping, creating mini-applications, or delivering complex Proofs of Concept (POC).

Trusted Advisor: Build deep relationships with C-level stakeholders—specifically the office of the CFO—offering mentorship and credible subject matter expertise.

Sales Partnership: Collaborate with Account Executives to lead RFP/RFI responses and develop winning value propositions

About You

You are an exceptional, dynamic presenter who thrives under the pressure of managing multiple simultaneous engagements.

You are a "continuous learner" who is passionate about innovation and creative problem-solving.

You are adaptable and excited to work in a high-growth, evolving organization.

Basic Qualifications

5+ years of experience in Finance Planning, Budgeting, and Forecasting systems. Consolidation experience is a must-have for this role.

3+ years’ experience and proven track record in a Presales Solution Consulting role, ideally within a software/SaaS environment.

3+ years extensive experience presenting to C-level executives with the ability to communicate influentially.

Strong understanding of the Financial Planning and Consolidation functional areas as well as Workforce Planning.

Other Qualifications:

Exposure to Planning solutions from Anaplan, Onestream, IBM Planning Analytics, SAP and Oracle would be an advantage

Experience engaging with medium and large enterprises across various industries such as FSI, Higher Education, Healthcare, Government, Professional Services and Retail.

Experience in Workforce, Headcount, Sales and Operational Planning will be desirable.

You are an exceptional, dynamic presenter with strong communication and influencing skills, specifically to a C-level Finance leadership audience

You possess credibility and passion for presentations and product demonstrations around FP&A technology solutions

You thrive under pressure and can manage multiple, simultaneous engagements with different prospects

You are a "continuous learner" who is passionate about innovation and creative problem-solving.

Applicants must have the legal right to work in Australia and willingness to travel up to 50% of the time to engage with customers and partners in the field.

You are adaptable and excited to work in a high-growth, evolving organization.

Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (or equivalent work experience).

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

Sims Limited (Sims) is a global leader in metal and electronics recycling, and an emerging leader in the renewable energy industry. With facilities across the globe, Sims plays an intrinsic role in the circular economy by making resources available for future use. As a responsible corporate citizen, we continuously seek new ways to broaden our participation in the environmental sector, ensuring that our future is as bright, safe, and secure as at any time in our long history.

With a promote-from-within philosophy and a variety of programmes available to support continuous learning, Sims offers the opportunity for a rewarding career. We are committed to the ecologically sound and sustainable use of resources and strive to operate in a manner that minimises waste and protects the environment.





The Role:

This is a full-time, high-impact role responsible for shaping how we assess, prioritise and track capital investments - ensuring our projects are financially sound, strategically aligned and delivered with rigour. You'll partner closely with operations, engineering and commercial teams, providing expert financial modelling, risk analysis and business case support for complex capital projects and M&A opportunities.

What you'll be responsible for:

Lead and support the development of high-quality business cases for complex capital projects
Build and review robust financial models (DCF, NPV, IRR, payback analysis)
Deliver clear, concise investment materials (presentations, written cases, financial analysis)
Contribute to short, medium and long-term capital budgeting processes
Support assessment of M&A opportunities, including modelling and due diligence input
Enhance and maintain a best-in-class capex reporting suite
Uplift organisational capability through coaching and guidance on business case development
Strengthen and embed transparent, rigorous investment approval processes

What you'll bring

Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics or STEM (minimum)
Strong financial modelling capability (including DCF and valuation fundamentals)
Experience in capital investment analysis and capital accounting concepts
Advanced Excel skills (PowerBI, SAP or similar systems experience highly regarded)
Ability to identify and quantify risks using scenario and sensitivity analysis
Excellent communication skills - able to translate complex analysis into clear recommendations.

What We Offer:

Paid Parental Leave - 12 weeks for primary carers, 6 weeks for secondary carers
Health Benefits - BUPA discounts for you and your family
Wellbeing Support - Access to a 24/7 Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Novated Lease- tax-effective vehicle leasing, providing both convenience and savings

Job Title: HR Data & Systems Analyst, contract with view to permanent
Ad Text:
About the Organisation:

Our client is a fast growing national organisation operating within a complex and highly regulated environment. Over recent years the business has experienced significant expansion and continues to invest in its people, systems, and operational capability to support sustained growth.

The organisation employs several hundred professionals across Australia and internationally and is known for its high performing culture, strong leadership capability, and professional working environment. The workforce consists of highly skilled specialists and senior professionals operating in a fast-paced commercial environment where data driven decision making and operational excellence are critical to business success.

The People and Culture function plays a central role in supporting this growth, partnering closely with executive leaders to ensure the organisation continues to attract, develop, and retain high performing talent.

About the Role:

This newly created HR Data and Systems Analyst role has been established to strengthen the organisation’s workforce analytics capability and improve the quality and accessibility of HR reporting and systems.

Working closely with the Head of HR and broader People and Culture team, you will play a key role in building and maintaining HR dashboards, analysing workforce data, and transforming people metrics into meaningful insights that support strategic decision making.

The role will also focus on optimising the organisation’s HR systems environment, ensuring data integrity and identifying opportunities to automate processes and improve reporting capability. You will work directly with senior stakeholders to deliver ad hoc analysis, executive level reports, and workforce insights that support organisational planning and performance.

This position sits within a lean and high performing HR team and will involve significant exposure to senior leaders across the organisation. The role offers an opportunity to build a modern HR analytics capability in a growing organisation where data and insights are becoming increasingly central to people and business decisions.

Key responsibilities include:

Developing and maintaining workforce analytics dashboards across key HR metrics including headcount, turnover, compliance reporting and remuneration insights.
Producing reports and workforce analysis for HR leadership and executive stakeholders.
Extracting, validating, and analysing HR data to identify trends and opportunities to improve organisational outcomes.
Supporting workforce planning analysis and organisational reporting requirements.
Maintaining and optimising HRIS platforms and ensuring ongoing data accuracy and integrity.
Identifying opportunities to automate HR processes and improve reporting through better use of system functionality and analytics tools.
Responding to ad hoc data requests from senior leaders and translating workforce data into clear insights and recommendations.
Supporting remuneration review analysis and reporting processes as required.
About You:

You are an analytically strong HR data professional who enjoys working with data to generate insights that influence decision making. You are comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders and translating complex workforce data into clear and meaningful narratives.

You bring strong technical capability combined with commercial awareness and strong communication skills. Importantly, you enjoy working in a hands on role within a growing organisation where you can shape how HR data is used and delivered.

You will bring:

Approximately 3 to 5 years experience in HR analytics, HR systems, workforce reporting or similar data focused roles.
Strong capability using Power BI, advanced Excel and data visualisation tools.
Experience working with HRIS platforms and maintaining system data integrity.
Strong understanding of workforce metrics including headcount, turnover, remuneration analysis and workforce planning.
The ability to analyse data, build reporting models, and present insights to non technical stakeholders.
A proactive mindset with the ability to work autonomously in a fast paced environment.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Relevant qualifications in business analytics, data science, information systems, mathematics or a related discipline will be highly regarded.
Why Apply?

This is a rare opportunity to build and shape HR analytics capability within a growing organisation where workforce data is becoming increasingly important to business decision making. You will join a collaborative, highly professional, progressive People and Culture team in a role that offers strong stakeholder exposure and the opportunity to influence how people insights are generated and delivered across the organisation.

How to Apply:

If you are confident that your experience reflects the above criteria, please apply online.

Should you have queries or would like to sense check your alignment, please contact Matthew Saitta on email matthew@tandempartners.com.au or call 02 8045 2923.

Tandem Partners is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Freelancer is the world's largest freelancing marketplace, and a thirteen-time Webby Award winner. It’s also home to some of the best product and engineering teams in Australia. At Freelancer, you won’t act on hunches, you won’t sacrifice quality for speed, and you won’t ship 100s of low-impact changes that users don’t actually care about.

Do you want to learn from great leaders who understand how the best product teams in the world operate? Do you want to uphold product practices that actually deliver value that customers will pay for? Do you want to become a product leader yourself?

We’re considering a range of both established and upcoming talent for this role. You will need to demonstrate the persistence needed to drive Growth, a passion for staying up-to-date with the latest Product and Strategy trends, and a knack for Discovery to learn what matters to customers.

Note — This role is full-time and on-site at our Sydney office. Check out the ‘Minimum Requirements’ (below) before applying.

What you’ll do as Growth Product Manager

Own the growth of a global marketplace with 80 million users.
Lead one or more core product teams to improve experiences on Freelancer, while also being a part of an overarching product function that spans acquisition, activation and retention.
Use both qualitative and quantitative research methods to identify, evaluate and set priorities for the most important problems worth solving, and validate potential solutions to ensure they are: (1) Valuable for customers, (2) viable for the business, (3) technologically feasible and scalable, and (4) easy to understand and use.
Collaborate with in-house experts to develop and execute on acquisition strategies, including SEO, SEM, paid social, brand, word-of-mouth, growth loops, etc. Together, you’ll lower acquisition costs, grow traffic volume and stability, diversify acquisition channels, and identify new channels that resonate with our key audiences.
Craft solutions in great detail, ensuring all important states and edge-cases are considered carefully, supplemented with well-written documentation containing all the info that designers and engineers need to succeed.
Collaborate directly with designers to (1) create intuitive experiences, from wire-frames to test-ready prototypes, (2) critically analyse every interaction and use-case to ensure UX-excellence, (3) act as the voice of the business and to ensure focus on objectives, and (4) act as the voice of the customer to ensure their diverse needs are met.
Collaborate directly with engineers to (1) help them resonate with users’ needs and struggles, (2) identify helpful technologies and possible solutions, (3) clearly define product requirements and translate them into precise technical solutions, and (4) hold each other accountable to only ship high-quality products and bug-free experiences. 
Grow a team that everybody wants to be a part of by (1) managing and mentoring engineers, (2) communicating an awesome product vision, (3) earning a reputation for high-quality work, (4) demonstrating a strong understanding of our tech-stack and the implications of technical decisions, and (5) facilitating collaboration with other product/functional teams.
Learn from and work with dedicated product-thinkers to develop product vision and strategy by understanding (1) where Freelancer should be strategically/competitively positioned in ~5 years times, (2) what ‘big bets’ should be taken/avoided, and (3) what order of execution is most effective.

Note for recent graduates

Are you are a high-performer who has graduated recently? If you have a reputation for excellence, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet all the ‘Minimum Requirements’ below. We tend to look for experience Product people, but we make exceptions for exceptional graduates. While the bar is set high, we don’t expect recent graduates to meet all of the criteria related to work experience.

Minimum Requirements

We will only consider applications that meet the following criteria:

You must have a degree in STEM, ideally from a reputable university, or equivalent qualification demonstrating your technical understanding. Alternatively, you may have strong skills with software (not vibe coding), or direct experience as a PM in an ‘Empowered Product Team’, or you may be a budding self-starter with an entrepreneurial reputation and one or two passion projects/products you’ve tried to grow.
You must be able to demonstrate your ability to use SQL, and foundational knowledge for querying large datasets efficiently. Alternatively, you may have strong skills using Python to manipulate data, with knowledge of various analytical methods and when to apply them to different types of data/problems.
You must have had experience in a role where you’ve been responsible for SEO, SEM, paid social, branding, or some other area of acquisition or marketing. Alternatively, you may be able to demonstrate extensive knowledge of at least one of these areas, potentially through involvement with trying to grow a startup or online community.
You must have a very keen interest in the world of Product, product management, product design, product strategy, product distribution, product growth, etc. You enjoy keeping in-touch with thought leaders in one of these spaces, or teaching yourself about how to make good products through some other means. You must be able to demonstrate what you’ve learned and discuss ideas with senior Product staff.

Additional Criteria

We will prioritise candidates who also meet some of the following criteria:

Examples of achieving significant user or revenue growth (or proven success executing a Product, Acquisition, SEO or Marketing strategy).
Experience in a role related to Product Management where you were responsible for building a real digital product, driving product discovery, working directly with designers and engineers, managing a team in a software environment, and/or analysing data for insights.
Experience using evidence to form hypotheses, designing experiments to test the validity your hypotheses, critically analysing A/B test results with a good understanding of basic statistical methods, and/or using insights to inform product decisions.

Bonus Points

Assuming you meet the ‘Minimum Criteria’ above, then these will also be helpful:

Examples of great communication skills used to articulate an idea, solicit advice from subject matter experts, respond productively to constructive feedback, and influence key stakeholders to achieve buy-in.
Examples of using design tools like Figma to visualise concepts, and iteratively incorporating user research or stakeholder feedback to progress from low-fidelity wireframes to high-fidelity prototypes.
Examples demonstrating your familiarity with two-sided marketplace dynamics, including how to leverage social network effects, data network effects, activation loops, growth strategies, expansion mechanics, etc.
Examples of trying to kickstart your own product, growing a community online, engaging with early-stage startups, contributing to educational events for sharing knowledge about digital product or business growth, or equivalent entrepreneurial activities.

What life is like at Freelancer:

True meritocracy — Your impact directly drives your trajectory. Our VP of Product started as a grad. Our Head of Support began as a CX agent. We promote from within and celebrate high performers.

Direct CEO engagement — We run weekly Town Halls with unfiltered Q&A. Your ideas reach the top without bureaucracy.

Biannual Hackathons — Two-day innovation sprints where teams ship real solutions, compete for prizes, and see their ideas go live (with real prize money on the line).

Premium Sydney HQ — Grosvenor Place with harbour views, plus weekly building events, complimentary wellness classes, activities, promotions, and deals.

Friday culture — Catered lunches featuring rotating global cuisines from local restaurants. We finish each week together.

Work hard, play hard — Yes, we have beer taps and a bar with killer views. We also once built a soccer stadium with two tonnes of turf. Because why not?

Fully stocked kitchens — Coffee, snacks, and everything you need to fuel long strategy sessions and growth sprints.

Ownership culture — Run your product's growth like it's your business. We reward those who think and act like owners. Become one of the many business and product leaders we’ve produced in the technology industry.

Do you have what it takes?

If you’re a go-getter who wants to level-up their career in product, and you meet the criteria above, we’d love to read your application. You won't just be optimising conversion rates for another SaaS app. You'll be connecting over 85 million people to economic opportunities across the globe. Every experiment you run, every feature you ship, every growth loop you unlock directly impacts someone's ability to earn a living, build a business, or change their life trajectory. Our platforms are powering commerce across 247 countries, regions, and territories. Your growth initiatives will literally help grow families, fund education, build businesses, and direct the future of the freelancing industry worldwide.

About the Role

We are looking for a Technical Business Analyst to act as the key bridge between business stakeholders and the AI, Data and Analytics teams. Working across strategic initiatives, operational improvements and product development cycles, this role will play a central part in shaping how the organisation uses data, analytics and emerging AI capabilities to support decision making and business outcomes.

The position sits within the AI, Data and Analytics team, which is currently building strong data foundations and governance practices before accelerating further data and AI initiatives over the coming years. The team currently includes Data Engineers, reporting developers and analytics specialists working across the organisation’s data platform and reporting environment.

This is a highly autonomous role where the Technical BA will operate as an extension of the business. Stakeholders across the organisation will rely on this person to capture ideas, structure requirements and help move initiatives forward from concept through to delivery.

The role also contributes to improving data literacy across the business, helping teams better understand how to interpret dashboards, work with analytics and use data to support better decision making.

Required Experience

Financial services experience, previous experience working with finance teams or a background in Novated Leasing/Fleet Management are all desirable but not essential.
5–8+ years’ experience as a Technical Business Analyst, ideally within data and analytics in enterprise digital transformation initiatives.
Ability to operate independently with strong autonomy and ownership, identifying opportunities and driving initiatives forward with minimal direction.
Strong stakeholder management couple with exceptional written and verbal communication skills across executive level, business and technical audiences.
Strong ability to translate business requirements into structured technical specifications for delivery teams.
Experience working within Agile environments, including backlog management and user story development.

Experience working with BI and reporting tools such as Power BI, Microsoft Fabric or similar platforms.

Experience working with cloud environments ideally Azure, but if not, AWS or GCP is beneficial.
Strong documentation and process modelling capability using tools such as Visio, Miro or Lucidchart.

•    Starting Salary $151,344 + 12.75% super
•    Free train travel to and from work on duty days
•    Permanent full time [with the opportunity to work flexibly at 0.8 FTE for the right candidate]
•    Flexible hours with 50/50 office and work from home arrangement
•    Be part of a high performing, inclusive team

About You
You’re a strategic and critical thinker with strong commercial acumen and advanced analytical capability. You don’t just analyse data — you challenge assumptions and translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations. You think critically, anticipate risks and identify workforce opportunities before they become issues. 

About the Role
We are seeking a highly strategic and analytical Principal to lead workforce planning and optimisation activities. These include developing functional and strategic workforce reports, modelling strategic workforce scenarios, and delivering performance driven workforce insights across the organisation.

This role is critical in translating complex data into actionable workforce strategies. It requires strong analysis of business objectives and plans, assessment of internal and external workforce risks, and identification of critical roles and capabilities required for the future.

Working in close partnership with Strategic People Partners (HR Business Partners), you will support leaders to identify workforce gaps and risks and provide strategic insight to mitigate these. You will lead the continuous improvement and increase the maturity of workforce planning activities across Queensland Rail.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Undertaking workforce planning activities, including (but not limited to)
environment, industry and horizon scanning, 
workforce data architecture and position governance,
providing advice on workforce risk identification and mitigation strategies.
Supporting the development and implementation of scalable workforce management practices leveraging workforce data and contemporary best practice.

•    Maintaining and continuously improving workforce planning frameworks, tools, and systems to ensure consistency, rigour and alignment across the organisation.
•    Ensuring workforce planning processes are clearly understood and underpinned by strong data accuracy and governance.
 
  
You’ll also bring the following: 
•    Advanced analytical capability, strong critical thinking skills, strong attention to detail as well as a proactive and solutions-focused mindset.
•    Extensive knowledge of contemporary organisational development and workforce planning methods.
•    High level of skill in the development and implementation of workforce planning frameworks, strategies, and models.
•    Experience using Microsoft Excel, PowerBI or Anaplan is advantageous.
•    Demonstrated ability to turn data into meaningful business insight.
 

Your journey matters. Make it with us.

Re.Group is an Australian-owned company focused on providing solutions for recycling and recovery of resources from waste. Our vision is to create a wasteless society—where all resources that become waste are reused and are not disposed.

At the heart of Re.Group is our focus on positive community impact. We collaborate with charities, private organisations, and the Government to benefit local communities.

We are seeking a Commercial Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will partner with Operations, Finance and Commercial teams to provide data-driven insights that support operational performance, strategic decision-making and continuous improvement across our network of recycling facilities.

This role suits someone who enjoys working with data, building models and translating analysis into practical business outcomes.

About the role

Partner with Operations, Finance and Commercial teams to gather inputs, challenge assumptions and maintain consistent model drivers across sites.

Analyse key operating metrics including throughput, labour productivity, downtime, maintenance performance and transport costs.

Identify performance improvement opportunities and quantify their P&L impact.

Develop scenario and sensitivity models across key business drivers.

Support the preparation of business cases for strategic projects including new technology investments and facility upgrades.

Link operational assumptions to financial outcomes including NPV, IRR and payback.

Build reporting and dashboards to track operational and financial performance.

Prepare clear insights and presentations for senior leaders explaining performance movements and recommended actions.

Work with finance teams to strengthen performance reporting and improve processes.

About you

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data.

Advanced Excel capability and experience with business intelligence tools such as Power BI or TM1.

Strong commercial mindset and ability to connect operational performance with financial outcomes.

Clear communication skills and the ability to work with stakeholders across multiple teams.

High attention to detail and accuracy in analysis and reporting.

Around 3+ years' experience in business analysis, financial planning or a related field.

Experience working with cross-functional teams such as operations, finance or IT.

Exposure to industries such as recycling, logistics, infrastructure or FMCG would be advantageous.

Why us?

Attractive salary package

Access to exclusive discounts and benefits at over 500 retailers across Australia

Novated car leasing

Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

Multiple career paths and advancement opportunities

The opportunity to work towards our vision of creating a wasteless society

Job Title: Head of Product, Free News & Lifestyle
Ad Text:
What's the role?

The Head of Product (Free News & Lifestyle) is a pivotal role responsible for managing the planning, development, and execution of the FNL brands digital product portfolio roadmap - covering the brands news.com.au, Kidspot, Escape and Body & Soul. Reporting to the General Manager - Product,  this role requires a deep understanding of the brands, their customers, user behaviour, and digital platforms. This position requires a blend of product management, strategy development, detailed planning, business acumen and people management.

Responsibilities

Product Strategy: Define and champion the long-term vision for all the FNL brands’ digital assets, ensuring it remains at the forefront of industry trends and user expectations.

Roadmap Development: Oversee the creation and execution of the FNL product brand roadmap, aligning it with brand and organisational strategies

Feature Prioritisation: Collaborate with stakeholders to identify and prioritise features that enhance user experience, engagement, and business goals.

Metrics Focus: Analyse product performance metrics and user feedback to drive continuous improvement

Innovation and Improvement: Continuously explore opportunities to enhance the products through innovation, ensuring it meets evolving consumer needs and market dynamics.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with stakeholders to align strategies, provide support, and drive innovation

Leadership: Manage small team of product managers

Who are you?

Vast experience in digital product management or a closely related field.

Proven track record of successfully delivering product roadmaps from strategy through to execution.

Experience managing and developing a team of direct reports.

Strong understanding of the digital media landscape and user behaviour.

Strategic and analytical mindset, using data and insights to guide product decisions.

Ability to collaborate across multiple teams and manage competing priorities while maintaining a strong focus on customer experience.

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

News Corp Australia is evolving in response to the expectations and challenges of the digital world in which we now live. Job Title: Lead Analyst - Line 2 Assurance, Enterprise Risk
Category:
Compliance & Risk (Banking & Financial Services)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Lead Assurance role within a recently established Line 2 Assurance team
Drive impactful change and uplift risk maturity across the organisation
Hybrid work environment; Max term contract until June 2027


About the business/role/team

The Lead Analyst sits within the Line 2 Assurance team in the Risk and Compliance Department. Reporting to the Senior Manager this role will participate in and take a lead on second line oversight and review activity as part of a highly capable and experienced team of risk and assurance professionals. The team executes various types of oversight and assurance activity to provide a view on the risk and control environment and opportunities for improvement and to support the bank-wide risk transformation underway.


About the role


Participate in and lead assurance-related engagements from planning through to communication of observations and recommendations to stakeholders.
Assess level of adoption of new risk standards and identify areas for improvement through regular and dynamic review and feedback.
Validate line 1 control self-assessment through a regular control assurance program of work.
Conduct targeted reviews across specific areas of risk / process to provide an in-depth view on the control environment.
Review of risk data and trends over time to identify themes and patterns.


About you


Proven experience in the assessment of internal controls to provide assurance that risks are controlled (can be through a line 1, line 2 or audit role).
Broad experience and knowledge of assurance methodologies, controls, and frameworks.
Experience across Technology and Cyber controls and related industry frameworks (COBIT, NIST, ISO etc) is a bonus.
Skills and experience in data and analysis to develop insights into the risk profile and to build and maintain an integrated view of controls' effectiveness and assurance activities.
Ability to build relationships across the organisation to become a trusted second-line risk partner.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in economics, finance, risk management, compliance, or a related discipline; or equivalent professional experience.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people.


Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go.


Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts.


We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

About Us 

Automic Group is Australia’s market leader in technology-driven investor administration. Combining proprietary cloud-native technology with expert service, Automic simplifies investor administration and governance for companies and funds. It provides the market's leading registry, employee share plans, IR analytics, and fund administration services for over 1,400 ASX-listed and unlisted organisations. Founded to challenge an industry reliant on outdated systems, Automic is committed to delivering smarter, more efficient, and secure solutions that help Australia’s companies and fund managers. 

Automic’s culture is collaborative, inclusive, and purpose-driven. It is grounded in our mission to connect companies and funds to their investors through technology, service, and trust, and guided by our values of One Automic, Go Beyond, Empowered, and Accountable. 




About the Role

Key Responsibilities

Act as a liaison between business teams, operations, technology, and external vendors.
Gather, analyse, and document business requirements (BRD, FRD and user stories).
Support implementation and enhancement of investment management systems, including configuration of system attributes, product setups, and reporting parameters.
Work across corporate actions, pricing validation, and client reporting processes.
Support testing cycles, including preparation, execution, and defect management.
Configure system attributes, product setups, and reporting parameters within InvestOne. 

Work across: 

RTK (Report Toolkit) for client reporting 
Corporate Actions lifecycle 
Pricing validation and control processes 
ABOR and MBOR environments 
Identify operational inefficiencies and recommend process improvements.



About you! 

To be successful in this role, you will have:

7–10 years in Financial Services, ideally in Asset or Wealth Management.
Strong understanding of the full trade lifecycle, multiple asset classes (Equities, Fixed Income, Derivatives, FX, Funds)
Knowledge of Fund Accounting principles (NAV, accruals, income, corporate actions) and Investment Operations, including reconciliations, exception handling, and break management.
Familiarity working with custodians, exchanges, or broker-dealers, and regulatory/client reporting environments is a plus.

Technical Skills & Tools

This is a compelling opportunity for a technology executive to lead enterprise technology, data insights capability and cyber strategy at a pivotal time of growth and transformation. This role will shape the roadmap to improve the guest experience, operational efficiency, data-driven decision-making, and cyber resilience across one of NSW's most-loved multi-site tourism organisations.


Why this Role Matters Now
A newly created role, this role sits at a genuine inflection point. Business intelligence and data insights, system integration, and enterprise architecture are all areas where the foundations are still being laid.  Reflections Holidays has a bold vision for the years ahead - increasing environmental sustainability, strengthening indigenous engagement, and improving the customer experience year after year. This is a unique opportunity to shape how technology, data, and digital capability underpin that ambition.


Leading a small and capable ICT service delivery and project team, the incoming CIO will have the mandate to embed a culture of continuous improvement where technology and data are used to drive measurable gains in efficiency and service delivery outcomes. You will establish a consistent KPI framework and deliver executive dashboards that are trusted, timely, and decision-ready.


You will build strong relationships with the CEO, Board, and Executive team, advising confidently on technology investment decisions and the future digital direction of the business.


About Reflections Holidays
Reflections Holidays are proud caretakers of the most idyllic holiday parks around NSW. Their parks are all located on iconic NSW Crown land, allowing them to create unforgettable experiences for their guests while nurturing the precious natural environment.


They give back to their local communities by reinvesting in their parks and reserves, helping maintain and protect these priceless natural treasures for current and future generations to enjoy. Managing over 9,000 hectares of Crown Land across NSW, they contribute over $100M annually to regional NSW economies.


Mother Nature is at the heart of what they do, their passion is to show Australians some of nature's best spots across NSW, whilst doing their best to protect and preserve it.


About You
You are an experienced technology executive and collaborative people leader with a genuine passion for working with the business to solve complex problems and build capability that ensures technology works for the business. You think strategically but stay grounded in delivery, and you know that the best outcomes come from earning trust across the whole organisation.


You bring a minimum of 8–10 years of senior ICT leadership experience, with a track record of developing and executing ICT strategy aligned with organisational objectives, leading organisational change and digital transformation initiatives, and delivering measurable results.


Your technical background spans enterprise systems, data, infrastructure, and vendor management, and you have proven experience in leading cybersecurity and risk management frameworks. You understand what good data governance looks like and have the commercial acumen to ensure a strong ROI on technology investment, including the ability to develop and manage CAPEX and OPEX budgets and present at CEO and Board level.


Naturally, you will have a Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business, or a related discipline.


Industry background that will set you apart
We will give strong preference to candidates who have operated in businesses with similar complexity and character to Reflections Holidays, particularly those with experience in:
Camping, accommodation, tourism, or hospitality — especially with a multi-site network of locations.
Retail or distribution businesses with high transaction volumes and operational technology complexity.
Sporting, leisure, or asset-intensive organisations with a distributed workforce and infrastructure.
Any multi-site operational network where technology connects people, places, and processes.
Next Steps
The benefits of working for Reflections Holidays include the opportunity to work on meaningful digital transformation initiatives, discounts on Reflections holidays, an attractive salary + bonus, and salary packaging options, including relocation assistance within Australia.


This position is based in Newcastle, and occasional travel across NSW will be required.  You’ll either be currently living in the area or be willing to locate to our beautiful region.   If you would like to know more, please feel free to reach out to Linda Apostolidis at linda@ntptalent.com.au for a confidential conversation or apply via the link.

Endgame Analytics (Endgame Economics Pty Ltd) is a boutique, technology-led consultancy specialising in providing expert mathematical and economic advice. Established in January 2021, we exist to empower our clients with the right information to drive the best outcomes.

Our Operations Research team focuses on building and deploying bespoke decision models for our clients. We tackle some of the most cutting-edge challenges in energy, scheduling, inventory management, and beyond. Our team is growing, and we are looking for someone who can help us in this journey.

THE ROLE

The successful candidate will work as a Consultant within our Operations Research Team as a senior subject-matter expert. In this role, you will work on analysing data, modelling systems, and suggesting actionable strategies to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance decision-making for a range of sectors, including aviation, media, telecommunication, energy and agriculture.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Your key responsibilities will include, but are not limited to:

Managing consulting engagements, working with the team to build a project roadmap and ensuring timely completion of client deliverables.

Writing clean and well-maintained code while upholding best practices and technical standards.

Performing code reviews to maintain the quality and scalability of code.

Evaluating business needs to determine the necessary system.

Running and developing mathematical optimisation models - both linear and mixed integer programs.

Providing expert advice in the fields of operations research, data science, and programming.

Building software or working with software engineering teams to deploy the models.

Preparing quantitative analysis, building models, and assisting in developing and documenting approaches to solving problems.

Preparing detailed reports and presentations summarising modelling results, insights, and recommendations for stakeholders such as senior management or regulatory bodies.

Preparation and review of clear, concise proposals to secure new business opportunities.

Collaborating with stakeholders to prioritise key features and define a clear delivery roadmap.

Managing relationships with clients.

Effectively escalating concerns and opportunities for business improvement to senior management.

ABOUT YOU

The ideal candidate must display the following skills and/or experience:

Tertiary education in either Computer Science, Engineering or other highly quantitative degree (e.g. statistics, computational biology, etc.).

3+ years of work experience in optimisation or other relevant fields.

Demonstrated deep understanding of media, retail and aviation sectors with successful deployment of a working optimisation model in this domain, is highly regarded.

A strong background in programming and grasp of the best software engineering practices.

Experience building large scale optimisation models that are performant and scale.

Experience building optimisation models in varied domains.

Familiarity with Gurobipy, CPLEX or other solvers.

A deep understanding of Linear Programming, Mixed Integer Programming and optimisation specific Heuristics (eg: Large Neighborhood Search).

Ability to learn programming languages or tools (softwares) quickly.

Past experience working in consulting, leading projects and presenting findings.

ABOUT US

We know that our people are our greatest asset. That’s why we invest in our team by fostering opportunities for growth, development, and recognition.

Learning & Development - From day one, you’ll be involved in important projects, gaining unique and highly valuable industry experience by working closely with senior staff. In addition, opportunities to interact directly with clients will allow you to expand your professional network.

Reward & Recognition - Benefit from strong progression pathways, and opportunities to expand your role as your knowledge and experience grows. Remuneration is competitive, and will be reviewed regularly, along with a generous bonus structure based on individual, team, and firm performance.

Flexibility & Autonomy - The two go hand in hand. Endgame advocates flexibility to balance work and personal life, as well as significant autonomy and ownership of your projects. This fosters professional growth and ensures your contributions have a meaningful impact.

Collaboration - Enhance and fine-tune your skills and expertise by working alongside leading technical experts with diverse experience across industries and skill sets.

OTHER DETAILS

Job type: Full-time, permanent

Salary: From AUD 100,000 (depending on skills and experience), plus superannuation.

Working closely with client SMEs, IT teams and system integrators, the role leads requirements gathering, testing and change activities across the employee lifecycle. 12–14 month engagement through June 2027, with potential extension
Opportunity to work on a large-scale Dayforce implementation

Work with client SMEs and third-party vendors to ensure Dayforce configuration meets business requirements

Support integration activities between Dayforce and existing operational systems

Experience facilitating requirements workshops and documenting design workbooks
Strong knowledge across end-to-end HCM processes including payroll and workforce management
Experience supporting UAT, testing strategy and test management

Process improvements

Motivated to improve processes proactively

Salary: Great salary plus 14% super and bonus potential
Ad Text:
At Australian Retirement Trust, we’re shaping better retirement outcomes for millions of Australians through meaningful digital experiences. Our Digital and Personalisation team is looking for an Optimisation Specialist (CRO) who thrives on data led testing, hands on delivery and turning insights into measurable impact, bringing strong front-end development capability and experimentation platform knowledge to design, build and deploy high-quality experiments across platforms such as Optimizely and Adobe Target.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As an Optimisation Specialist (CRO), you’ll be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of onsite A/B and multivariate experiments that improve member experience and conversion across our digital platforms.  Acting as both an experimentation lead and front-end delivery specialist, you’ll translate hypotheses into robust, scalable tests that deliver insights and measurable performance.


You’ll design and deliver a structured experimentation roadmap aligned to our personalisation strategy, working closely with analytics, on‑site, and broader digital teams. This is a hands‑on role with a strong technical component, where you’ll use your knowledge of experimentation platforms and confidence in HTML, CSS and JavaScript to build, implement and QA experiment variations to production standard.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Designing and maintaining a test and experimentation roadmap aligned to personalisation and optimisation strategy.
Creating clear test documentation including hypotheses, success metrics, and setup requirements.
Building and executing A/B and multivariate tests using tools such as Optimizely or Adobe Target.
Completing hands‑on front‑end development (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) to implement and quality‑assure experiments.
Partnering with analytics teams on pre‑test sizing, post‑test analysis, and insight generation.
Communicating results, learnings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business.
Supporting ideation, prioritisation and quarterly planning cadences for optimisation initiatives.
Contributing to experimentation frameworks and maintaining documentation of tests and outcomes.
Ensuring all experimentation aligns with governance and members’ best financial interests.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You have proven experience designing, executing and analysing onsite experiments, including A/B and multivariate testing.
You’re confident using CRO and testing platforms such as Optimizely or Adobe Target.
You bring strong front‑end development skills, with hands‑on experience in HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
You’re comfortable using data and insights to inform test design, prioritisation and decision‑making.
You’ve partnered closely with analytics teams to analyse results and translate findings into clear recommendations.
You communicate confidently and can engage a range of stakeholders with test outcomes and learnings.
You hold a tertiary qualification in Marketing, Digital, Technology or a related field, or you bring equivalent practical experience.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close COB Wednesday, 18 March 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.
