About PerData

PerData is a trusted Australian technology partner specialising in transforming complex data into actionable insights. With over 20 years of experience, we collaborate with leading organisations across corporate, health and government sectors to modernise and manage their data systems. 

Our expertise spans Business Intelligence, Data Governance, Enterprise Architecture and Managed Services, underpinned by a commitment to making technology more bankable, adaptable and secure. 

Position Overview

As a Senior Business Intelligence Consultant at PerData, you will play a pivotal role in helping our clients harness the power of their data. Your expertise will drive strategic decision-making and operational efficiency through the development and implementation of BI solutions. 

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, patterns and insights that inform business strategies. Develop and maintain dashboards and reports underpinned by relevant data models.

BI Solution Development: Design and implement business intelligence solutions using tools like Power BI, ensuring they align with client objectives and enhance decision-making processes.

Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams and clients to understand their data needs, translating business requirements into technical specifications.

Data Quality Assurance: Ensure the accuracy, integrity and consistency of data across various sources, implementing best practices for data governance.

Continuous Improvement: Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging technologies such as AI, recommending and integrating innovative solutions to enhance BI capabilities.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, information technology or a related field. Relevant Microsoft certifications would be an advantage.

Experience: Minimum of 7+ years in business intelligence or data analytics roles, with a proven track record of delivering BI solutions.

Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in SQL for data querying and manipulation. Proficiency in Power BI and related Microsoft platforms, such as data warehousing and ETL technologies.

Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with attention to detail and a focus on delivering actionable insights.

Communication Skills: Excellent client facing verbal and written communication skills, capable of presenting complex data insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Business Acumen: Understanding of business operations and strategy, particularly in financial modelling, with the ability to align analytical efforts with organisational goals. 

Benefits

Competitive Salary: Attractive remuneration package commensurate with experience.

Professional Development: Access to continuous learning opportunities and certifications in BI tools and methodologies.

Collaborative Environment: Work within a dynamic and supportive team that values innovation and knowledge sharing.

Work-Life Balance: Flexible working arrangements to support personal well-being and productivity.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer - 12 Month Max Term Contract
Ad Text:
A great opportunity to work with one of Australia’s largest general insurers

Be part of a collaborative and innovative data team

Open to candidates across Sydney, Melbourne and Brisbane - Remote / Hybrid role

About Hollard   

Hollard Insurance is part of the Hollard Group of Companies and one of Australia’s largest general insurers Australia and New Zealand. We underwrite a broad range of general insurance products, including motor, home, contents, business, bicycle and pet, in proud partnership with some of Australia's biggest consumer brands and broker businesses. As an independent and privately owned company, we believe we can go further together – for our people, customers, communities and partners – through an innovative approach to providing insurance. 

Our state-of-the-art facilities in North Sydney reflect our commitment to providing innovative workspaces that support hybrid and remote work. 

Your Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will work in the Data team and actively collaborate in our data journey, providing significant data engineering direction for the delivery of our Azure Data Platform. You will build and maintain data pipelines to ingest data into the Data Platform and support integration into other systems.

Your duties and responsibilities

Maintain, support, and monitor existing production Azure Synapse data engineering pipelines, PySpark notebooks, data platform lake house architecture and Azure SQL ODS storage and Stored Procedures, CI/CD pipelines to make sure all data loads on data platform meet data quality standards and business SLA requirements.

Participate and contribute to Azure data platform architecture design, data modelling, gathering and analysis of data requirements; understand document, communicate and build appropriate solutions.

Participate and contribute to the end-to-end data flow in the Azure data platform – ensuring data quality, efficient processing, and timely delivery of accurate and trusted data.

Coach and mentor data engineers on Azure technologies.

Collaborate and work closely with team members and contribute significantly to building a high performing, collaborative, transparent, and result-driven data engineering team.

About You

Extensive experience in design, build and support of data engineering pipelines in the data warehousing, BI analytics, and/or ML space is required.

Substantial work experience using Azure data analytics tool such as Data Factory, Synapse, Azure SQL, data lake gen2, key vault is required.

Proficiency in PySpark and Apache Spark with Python is required.

Experience with CI/CD using modern DevOps / Data Ops integrated processes is desired.

Exposure to Snowflake platform support and BAU operations, including query optimization, cost governance via warehouse management, and automated monitoring of ingestion pipelines.

Implemented a unified data platform using Microsoft Fabric, migrating existing Synapse pipelines to Fabric Data Factory

Experience of manipulating semi-structured data (XML, JSON, Parquet)

Working at Hollard   

At Hollard, you'll discover an environment where you can truly make an impact. We offer the best of both worlds – the opportunity, agility and creativity of a company growing with scale, alongside a focus on becoming a catalyst for enduring, positive, social change.

We're a tight-knit team, where authentic voices and unique perspectives matter. We work supportively, collaboratively and inclusively to help shape our future and create shared value for our communities.

Hollard proudly promotes flexible work including remote work, non-traditional work patterns, and work-family balance. We're also unapologetically committed to creating a workplace that values your diversity with a true sense of inclusion. We actively seek to hire for your different lived experiences, including as it relates to disability, ethnicity, faith, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, gender diversity, sexual orientation, age, neurodiversity.

We're building something special. Working closely with our industry partners to reshape insurance for the better, we're people changing insurance.

Please note: Even if you don't meet all our selection criteria, we encourage you to apply.

A full background check will be completed on the successful applicant.

Australian work rights are required for this position.

Visit the Hollard careers page to learn about how Hollard will collect, store and handle your personal information.

We are NOT engaging Recruitment Agencies for this role and ask agencies to respect our policy of no unsolicited contact regarding advertised vacancies.

Make a meaningful difference with us:

We want people who are great as individuals, and even better in a team.

We’re on the lookout for a strategic and hands-on Solution Analysis & Design Manager, who’s ready to roll up their sleeves and lead the delivery of high-quality solution analysis and project-level solution architecture across enterprise-wide digital and technology initiatives. Operating within our Digital & Technology (D&T) Transformation function, this role is responsible for ensuring solution designs are aligned with Enterprise Architecture principles, governance standards and UnitingCare’s 2030 Strategy.

This role will lead and guide project and program analysis and design resources (including Solution Architects, Business Analysts, technical leads and vendors), ensuring design standards, guardrails and assurance processes are consistently applied to deliver sustainable, customer-centric technology outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Own the delivery of project-level solution architecture and detailed design execution for approved initiatives, ensuring solution quality, technical coherence and alignment with delivery plans.

Ensure all solution designs are reviewed through established architecture governance and design assurance forums, addressing feedback and demonstrating compliance with enterprise architecture principles and standards.

Lead and guide D&T project and program analysis and design resources with respect to scope, deliverables, design artefacts, review processes and expected outcomes.

Partner with the Enterprise Architecture Manager to support demand intake, triage and early assessment activities, contributing feasibility input and delivery insights to inform investment decisions.

Develop and implement analysis and design standards, templates and guidance to ensure fit-for-purpose, customer-centric solution outcomes.

Drive continuous improvement of technical delivery methods, reference architectures, cost estimation models, policies and standards.

Maintain oversight of solution architecture capacity, demand and performance reporting.

Prepare senior-level presentations and reports to align stakeholders and drive informed decision-making.

Analyse industry trends, product lifecycles and technology roadmaps to support sustainable digital blueprint delivery.

Drive successful implementation of D&T initiatives by translating between technology and business stakeholders.

 

What you will bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Management or related discipline (or equivalent industry experience).

7+ years’ experience leading a solution analysis and design capability within complex, multi-vendor environments.

Extensive experience in business requirements gathering, high-level and detailed systems design, and implementable architecture patterns.

Demonstrated experience establishing and maintaining solution governance and design assurance processes.

Strong background across solution architecture, business analysis and/or software development.

Exceptional analytical, conceptual and problem-solving skills.

Proven ability to engage executive and technical stakeholders with high-quality presentations, reports and documentation.

Experience working across SaaS platforms, cloud (Azure), integration environments and multi-vendor ecosystems highly regarded.

Relevant certifications (TOGAF, ITIL, Agile or vendor certifications) desirable.

A collaborative leadership style, strong mentoring capability and commitment to developing technical talent.

Commitment to the mission and values of the not-for-profit sector.

 

Why you will love it here:

Working with us means stepping into a role that’s as broad as it is meaningful. You will gain exposure to:

Multiple industries under one roof - health, aged care, disability, community services, and more.

A wide range of workforce models, leadership styles, and organisational challenges.

Opportunities to stretch, grow, and shape your career in ways you won’t find elsewhere.

 

And the perks? We’ve got plenty:

Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.

Access to salary packaging, meal and entertainment benefits - up to $15,900 per year tax free as well as up to $2,650 of your pre-tax income to pay for meal and entertainment benefits. To learn more about this benefit watch the video via the following link: Salary Packaging Explained.     

A recognition and rewards platform, providing employee discounts at hundreds of retailers, including everyday expenses, holidays, health insurance, a wellbeing program and employee assistance program.

Career development opportunities to challenge yourself, grow and make a meaningful difference.

12 weeks paid parental leave and 2 weeks paid partner leave, available after 6 months service, in addition to the government scheme.

Diversity and inclusive leave, offering paid gender affirmation leave and cultural leave options (paid and unpaid) for those who may celebrate Lunar New Year, Diwali, Ramadan, NAIDOC weeks and other significant cultural events.

We’re committed to creating safe, inclusive, and welcoming environments for all—especially children, young people, and the elderly. Our services are built on trust, care, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Ready to bring your whole self to work and make a real difference? Apply now and join a team where your work truly matters.

Head of Consumer Growth

Sydney | High-Growth FinTech | Strategic Leadership Role

A rare opportunity to join one of Australia’s fastest-growing fintechs that is reshaping how consumers access finance.

This founder-led, ASX-listed business has built a reputation for challenging traditional players through smart technology, better customer experiences and a relentless focus on innovation.

 Since launching, the company has continuously scaled while continuing to take meaningful market share across their category.

With a sizeable but nimble team in place, the business sits in the sweet spot of being large enough to have real market impact, yet small and agile enough for individuals to genuinely influence strategy and outcomes.

As growth accelerates, they are now seeking a Head of Consumer Growth to lead and scale one of the company’s most important revenue channels.

The Opportunity
A part of the Exec leadership team, this role owns the end-to-end strategy and performance of the direct-to-consumer channel.

You will take full commercial ownership of the digital channel, defining the growth roadmap, optimising the customer journey, and leading a multidisciplinary team across marketing, sales and digital.

This role combines strategy, growth leadership and P&L accountability, making it ideal for someone who thrives in high-growth, data-driven environments.

Key Responsibilities
Strategy & P&L Ownership

Define and execute the direct-to-consumer strategy aligned with broader business objectives


Own the P&L for the digital channel and drive sustainable revenue growth
Growth & Customer Acquisition

Lead customer acquisition strategy across digital marketing and performance channels


Optimise the conversion funnel to maximise ROI and deliver against ambitious growth targets
Customer Journey & Value Optimisation

Continuously improve conversion, retention and customer lifetime value


Use data-driven segmentation and advanced analytics to drive cross-sell, upsell and refinance opportunities
Leadership & Team Development

Lead and grow a multidisciplinary team across growth marketing, sales and digital optimisation


Establish strong performance frameworks, KPIs and capability development pathways
Product & Market Innovation

Identify opportunities for new products, pricing strategies and channel expansion


Lead competitive analysis and market insights to support innovation and growth
Strategic Partnerships

Manage and grow key strategic partnerships, including major financial institutions and distribution partners
Technology & Customer Experience

Oversee the MarTech stack and continuously improve the digital application journey
Risk, Compliance & Operations

Partner closely with risk, compliance and operations teams to ensure responsible lending practices while improving efficiency and customer outcomes
About You
You are a commercially minded digital leader who combines strategic thinking with hands-on execution.

You thrive in high-growth environments, enjoy building and leading teams, and have a strong track record of delivering measurable outcomes through digital channels.

You will demonstrate:

Deep B2C experience across digital growth, product, sales leadership, partnerships or portfolio management


A Proven ability to design and optimise customer journeys and conversion funnels


Strong commercial acumen with experience owning revenue, budgets or P&L


Experience leading cross-functional teams spanning marketing, product and sales


Exceptional stakeholder management skills and the ability to influence across an organisation


Experience in fintech, consumer lending or financial services is highly regarded, though leaders from other high-growth direct-to-consumer businesses may also be considered.
You’ll be joining a high-performing, collaborative and ambitious team that is building one of Australia’s most innovative fintech platforms.

The culture is entrepreneurial and outcome-focused, with a strong emphasis on ownership, innovation and continuous improvement.

You Will Benefit From:

The opportunity to shape a critical revenue channel in a scaling fintech


Working with a smart, ambitious and collaborative team


A culture that encourages innovation and new ideas


Flexible working arrangements


Professional development and learning opportunities
If you’re excited by the opportunity to lead digital growth in a high-performing fintech and play a key role in shaping their growth strategy, I would love to hear from you.

Apply now or call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 0413 463 043.

Ad Text:
What you'll do  

Develop, implement and maintain the Service Portfolio strategy and implementation plan in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring digital services meet the needs of Queensland Health and the community.
Lead service portfolio planning, prioritisation, funding, and approval processes in collaboration with the Digital Asset Strategy and Portfolio team.
Identify and manage risks within the service portfolio, ensuring compliance with relevant legislation, policies, and standards.
Develop, implement, and maintain service governance policies, procedures, and lifecycle management standards.
Provide regular reporting and insights on service portfolio performance, risks, and value realisation to executive and governance bodies.
Monitor the IT Service Portfolio and develop metrics to track consumption, quality, availability, sustainability, and benefits realisation.

About you  

We are searching for someone who has:

Demonstrated broad knowledge in current and emerging technologies, applications and “productization” of services and their life-cycle management (SFIA Skill: EMRG – Emerging technologies monitoring level 5,)
Proven ability to drive the design and development of services and/or products within a large, diverse, and complex organisation by applying good practice- including, COBIT, ITIl (SFIA Skill: PROD – Product management level 5)
Ability to provide specialist advice relating to service portfolio planning, status, performance, prioritisation, funding, and approval to inform investment decision making (SFIA Skill : TECH – Specialist Advice level 4) (NHS: Effective and strategic influencing)
Proven ability to provide strategic direction that enables the delivery of sustainable, value for money, fit for purpose services and technologies that meet the business requirements of customers (SKIA Skill: ITSP – Strategic Planning level 5)
Demonstrated analytical and interpersonal skills commensurate with managing a complex and fast evolving service delivery organisation, including the ability to communicate with clarity and influence across a broad range of internal and external stakeholders (SFIA Skill: CNSL – Consultancy level 5)

Why work with us?  

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. 

Competitive salaries 

12.75% superannuation  
17.5% leave loading 

Employee wellbeing & development 

Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members 
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave. 
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.  
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development. 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion  

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.  

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.  

Ready to apply? 

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.
